1262.             <summary>
1263.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> class.
1264.             </summary>
1265.             <param name="innerClient">The underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, or the next instance in a chain of clients.</param>
1266.             <param name="loggerFactory">An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory"/> to use for logging information about function invocation.</param>
1267.             <param name="functionInvocationServices">An optional <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> to use for resolving services required by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances being invoked.</param>
1268.         </member>
1269.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.Processor">
1270.             <summary>Gets the function invocation processor, creating it lazily.</summary>
1271.         </member>
1272.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.CurrentContext">
1273.             <summary>
1274.             Gets or sets the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvocationContext"/> for the current function invocation.
1275.             </summary>
1276.             <remarks>
1277.             This value flows across async calls.
1278.             </remarks>
1279.         </member>
1280.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.IncludeDetailedErrors">
1281.             <summary>
1282.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether detailed exception information should be included
1283.             in the chat history when calling the underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>.
1284.             </summary>
1285.             <value>
1286.             <see langword="true"/> if the full exception message is added to the chat history
1287.             when calling the underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>.
1288.             <see langword="false"/> if a generic error message is included in the chat history.
1289.             The default value is <see langword="false"/>.
1290.             </value>
1291.             <remarks>
1292.             <para>
1293.             Setting the value to <see langword="false"/> prevents the underlying language model from disclosing
1294.             raw exception details to the end user, since it doesn't receive that information. Even in this
1295.             case, the raw <see cref="T:System.Exception"/> object is available to application code by inspecting
1296.             the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent.Exception"/> property.
1297.             </para>
1298.             <para>
1299.             Setting the value to <see langword="true"/> can help the underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> bypass problems on
1300.             its own, for example by retrying the function call with different arguments. However it might
1301.             result in disclosing the raw exception information to external users, which can be a security
1302.             concern depending on the application scenario.
1303.             </para>
1304.             <para>
1305.             Changing the value of this property while the client is in use might result in inconsistencies
1306.             as to whether detailed errors are provided during an in-flight request.
1307.             </para>
1308.             </remarks>
1309.         </member>
1310.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation">
1311.             <summary>
1312.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether to allow concurrent invocation of functions.
1313.             </summary>
1314.             <value>
1315.             <see langword="true"/> if multiple function calls can execute in parallel.
1316.             <see langword="false"/> if function calls are processed serially.
1317.             The default value is <see langword="false"/>.
1318.             </value>
1319.             <remarks>
1320.             An individual response from the inner client might contain multiple function call requests.
1321.             By default, such function calls are processed serially. Set <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> to
1322.             <see langword="true"/> to enable concurrent invocation such that multiple function calls can execute in parallel.
1323.             </remarks>
1324.         </member>
1325.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest">
1326.             <summary>
1327.             Gets or sets the maximum number of iterations per request.
1328.             </summary>
1329.             <value>
1330.             The maximum number of iterations per request.
1331.             The default value is 40.
1332.             </value>
1333.             <remarks>
1334.             <para>
1335.             Each request to this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> might end up making
1336.             multiple requests to the inner client. Each time the inner client responds with
1337.             a function call request, this client might perform that invocation and send the results
1338.             back to the inner client in a new request. This property limits the number of times
1339.             such a roundtrip is performed. The value must be at least one, as it includes the initial request.
1340.             </para>
1341.             <para>
1342.             Changing the value of this property while the client is in use might result in inconsistencies
1343.             as to how many iterations are allowed for an in-flight request.
1344.             </para>
1345.             </remarks>
1346.         </member>
1347.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumConsecutiveErrorsPerRequest">
1348.             <summary>
1349.             Gets or sets the maximum number of consecutive iterations that are allowed to fail with an error.
1350.             </summary>
1351.             <value>
1352.             The maximum number of consecutive iterations that are allowed to fail with an error.
1353.             The default value is 3.
1354.             </value>
1355.             <remarks>
1356.             <para>
1357.             When function invocations fail with an exception, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>
1358.             continues to make requests to the inner client, optionally supplying exception information (as
1359.             controlled by <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.IncludeDetailedErrors"/>). This allows the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> to
1360.             recover from errors by trying other function parameters that might succeed.
1361.             </para>
1362.             <para>
1363.             However, in case function invocations continue to produce exceptions, this property can be used to
1364.             limit the number of consecutive failing attempts. When the limit is reached, the exception will be
1365.             rethrown to the caller.
1366.             </para>
1367.             <para>
1368.             If the value is set to zero, all function calling exceptions immediately terminate the function
1369.             invocation loop and the exception will be rethrown to the caller.
1370.             </para>
1371.             <para>
1372.             Changing the value of this property while the client is in use might result in inconsistencies
1373.             as to how many iterations are allowed for an in-flight request.
1374.             </para>
1375.             </remarks>
1376.         </member>
1377.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools">
1378.             <summary>Gets or sets a collection of additional tools the client is able to invoke.</summary>
1379.             <remarks>
1380.             These will not impact the requests sent by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>, which will pass through the
1381.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools" /> unmodified. However, if the inner client requests the invocation of a tool
1382.             that was not in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools" />, this <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/> collection will also be consulted
1383.             to look for a corresponding tool to invoke. This is useful when the service might have been preconfigured to be aware
1384.             of certain tools that aren't also sent on each individual request.
1385.             </remarks>
1386.         </member>
1387.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.TerminateOnUnknownCalls">
1388.             <summary>Gets or sets a value indicating whether a request to call an unknown function should terminate the function calling loop.</summary>
1389.             <value>
1390.             <see langword="true"/> to terminate the function calling loop and return the response if a request to call a tool
1391.             that isn't available to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> is received; <see langword="false"/> to create and send a
1392.             function result message to the inner client stating that the tool couldn't be found. The default is <see langword="false"/>.
1393.             </value>
1394.             <remarks>
1395.             <para>
1396.             When <see langword="false"/>, call requests to any tools that aren't available to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>
1397.             will result in a response message automatically being created and returned to the inner client stating that the tool couldn't be
1398.             found. This behavior can help in cases where a model hallucinates a function, but it's problematic if the model has been made aware

@@ -1203,248 +1203,248 @@
         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient">
             <summary>
             A delegating chat client that invokes functions defined on <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/>.
             Include this in a chat pipeline to resolve function calls automatically.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             When this client receives a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in a chat response from its inner
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, it responds by invoking the corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> defined
             in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/> (or in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/>), producing a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
             that it sends back to the inner client. This loop is repeated until there are no more function calls to make, or until
             another stop condition is met, such as hitting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest"/>.
             </para>
             <para>
             If a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration"/> but not an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>, the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not attempt to invoke it, and instead allow that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/>
             to pass back out to the caller. It is then that caller's responsibility to create the appropriate <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
             for that call and send it back as part of a subsequent request.
             </para>
             <para>
             Further, if a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ApprovalRequiredAIFunction"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not
             attempt to invoke it directly. Instead, it will replace that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> with a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ToolApprovalRequestContent"/>
             that wraps the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> and indicates that the function requires approval before it can be invoked. The caller is then
             responsible for responding to that approval request by sending a corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ToolApprovalResponseContent"/> in a subsequent
             request. The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will then process that approval response and invoke the function as appropriate.
             </para>
             <para>
             Due to the nature of interactions with an underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, if any <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> is received
             for a function that requires approval, all received <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in that same response will also require approval,
             even if they were not <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ApprovalRequiredAIFunction"/> instances. If this is a concern, consider requesting that multiple tool call
             requests not be made in a single response, by setting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowMultipleToolCalls"/> to <see langword="false"/>.
             </para>
             <para>
             A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> instance is thread-safe for concurrent use so long as the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances employed as part of the supplied <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> are also safe.
             The <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> property can be used to control whether multiple function invocation
             requests as part of the same request are invocable concurrently, but even with that set to <see langword="false"/>
             (the default), multiple concurrent requests to this same instance and using the same tools could result in those
             tools being used concurrently (one per request). For example, a function that accesses the HttpContext of a specific
             ASP.NET web request should only be used as part of a single <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> at a time, and only with
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> set to <see langword="false"/>, in case the inner client decided to issue multiple
             invocation requests to that same function.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient._currentContext">
             <summary>The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvocationContext"/> for the current function invocation.</summary>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.FunctionInvocationServices">
             <summary>Gets the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> specified when constructing the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>, if any.</summary>
         </member>
         <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient._logger">
             <summary>The logger to use for logging information about function invocation.</summary>
         </member>
         <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient._activitySource">
             <summary>The <see cref="T:System.Diagnostics.ActivitySource"/> to use for telemetry.</summary>
             <remarks>This component does not own the instance and should not dispose it.</remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,System.IServiceProvider)">
             <summary>
             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> class.
             </summary>
             <param name="innerClient">The underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, or the next instance in a chain of clients.</param>
             <param name="loggerFactory">An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory"/> to use for logging information about function invocation.</param>
             <param name="functionInvocationServices">An optional <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> to use for resolving services required by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances being invoked.</param>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.Processor">
             <summary>Gets the function invocation processor, creating it lazily.</summary>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.CurrentContext">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvocationContext"/> for the current function invocation.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             This value flows across async calls.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.IncludeDetailedErrors">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets a value indicating whether detailed exception information should be included
             in the chat history when calling the underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>.
             </summary>
             <value>
             <see langword="true"/> if the full exception message is added to the chat history
             when calling the underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>.
             <see langword="false"/> if a generic error message is included in the chat history.
             The default value is <see langword="false"/>.
             </value>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             Setting the value to <see langword="false"/> prevents the underlying language model from disclosing
             raw exception details to the end user, since it doesn't receive that information. Even in this
             case, the raw <see cref="T:System.Exception"/> object is available to application code by inspecting
             the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent.Exception"/> property.
             </para>
             <para>
             Setting the value to <see langword="true"/> can help the underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> bypass problems on
             its own, for example by retrying the function call with different arguments. However it might
             result in disclosing the raw exception information to external users, which can be a security
             concern depending on the application scenario.
             </para>
             <para>
             Changing the value of this property while the client is in use might result in inconsistencies
             as to whether detailed errors are provided during an in-flight request.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets a value indicating whether to allow concurrent invocation of functions.
             </summary>
             <value>
             <see langword="true"/> if multiple function calls can execute in parallel.
             <see langword="false"/> if function calls are processed serially.
             The default value is <see langword="false"/>.
             </value>
             <remarks>
             An individual response from the inner client might contain multiple function call requests.
             By default, such function calls are processed serially. Set <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> to
             <see langword="true"/> to enable concurrent invocation such that multiple function calls can execute in parallel.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the maximum number of iterations per request.
             </summary>
             <value>
             The maximum number of iterations per request.
             The default value is 40.
             </value>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             Each request to this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> might end up making
             multiple requests to the inner client. Each time the inner client responds with
             a function call request, this client might perform that invocation and send the results
             back to the inner client in a new request. This property limits the number of times
             such a roundtrip is performed. The value must be at least one, as it includes the initial request.
             </para>
             <para>
             Changing the value of this property while the client is in use might result in inconsistencies
             as to how many iterations are allowed for an in-flight request.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumConsecutiveErrorsPerRequest">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the maximum number of consecutive iterations that are allowed to fail with an error.
             </summary>
             <value>
             The maximum number of consecutive iterations that are allowed to fail with an error.
             The default value is 3.
             </value>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             When function invocations fail with an exception, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>
             continues to make requests to the inner client, optionally supplying exception information (as
             controlled by <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.IncludeDetailedErrors"/>). This allows the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> to
             recover from errors by trying other function parameters that might succeed.
             </para>
             <para>
             However, in case function invocations continue to produce exceptions, this property can be used to
             limit the number of consecutive failing attempts. When the limit is reached, the exception will be
             rethrown to the caller.
             </para>
             <para>
             If the value is set to zero, all function calling exceptions immediately terminate the function
             invocation loop and the exception will be rethrown to the caller.
             </para>
             <para>
             Changing the value of this property while the client is in use might result in inconsistencies
             as to how many iterations are allowed for an in-flight request.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools">
             <summary>Gets or sets a collection of additional tools the client is able to invoke.</summary>
             <remarks>
             These will not impact the requests sent by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>, which will pass through the
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools" /> unmodified. However, if the inner client requests the invocation of a tool
             that was not in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools" />, this <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/> collection will also be consulted
             to look for a corresponding tool to invoke. This is useful when the service might have been preconfigured to be aware
             of certain tools that aren't also sent on each individual request.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.TerminateOnUnknownCalls">
             <summary>Gets or sets a value indicating whether a request to call an unknown function should terminate the function calling loop.</summary>
             <value>
             <see langword="true"/> to terminate the function calling loop and return the response if a request to call a tool
             that isn't available to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> is received; <see langword="false"/> to create and send a
             function result message to the inner client stating that the tool couldn't be found. The default is <see langword="false"/>.
             </value>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             When <see langword="false"/>, call requests to any tools that aren't available to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>
             will result in a response message automatically being created and returned to the inner client stating that the tool couldn't be
             found. This behavior can help in cases where a model hallucinates a function, but it's problematic if the model has been made aware
             of the existence of tools outside of the normal mechanisms, and requests one of those. <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/> can be used
             to help with that. But if instead the consumer wants to know about all function call requests that the client can't handle,
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.TerminateOnUnknownCalls"/> can be set to <see langword="true"/>. Upon receiving a request to call a function
             that the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> doesn't know about, it will terminate the function calling loop and return
             the response, leaving the handling of the function call requests to the consumer of the client.
             </para>
             <para>
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool"/>s that the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> is aware of (for example, because they're in
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/> or <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/>) but that aren't <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>s aren't considered
             unknown, just not invocable. Any requests to a non-invocable tool will also result in the function calling loop terminating,
             regardless of <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.TerminateOnUnknownCalls"/>.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.FunctionInvoker">
             <summary>Gets or sets a delegate used to invoke <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances.</summary>
             <remarks>

             invoking the instance and returning its result. If this delegate is set to a non-<see langword="null"/> value,
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.InvokeFunctionAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvocationContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> will replace its normal invocation with a call to this delegate, enabling
             this delegate to assume all invocation handling of the function.

cat > /tmp/agent_api_summary.txt << 'EOF'
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
MICROSOFT AGENTS.AI FRAMEWORK - PUBLIC API SURFACE FOR AI AGENT LOOP WITH TOOL CALLING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## CORE AGENT ABSTRACTIONS (Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions v1.3.0)

### 1. AIAgent (Abstract Base Class)
Location: Microsoft.Agents.AI namespace
File: /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/1.3.0/lib/net10.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions.dll

PROPERTIES:
  - Id (string): Unique identifier for agent instance
  - Name (string, nullable): Human-readable name
  - Description (string, nullable): Agent's purpose/capabilities
  - CurrentRunContext (AgentRunContext): Current agent run context (flows across async)
  - IdCore (string, nullable): Custom identifier (virtual)

CORE METHODS:
  - CreateSessionAsync(CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentSession>
    Creates a new conversation session compatible with this agent
  
  - RunAsync() - Multiple overloads:
    • RunAsync(AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
      (Run with no message, using existing context)
    
    • RunAsync(string message, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
      (Run with text message from user)
    
    • RunAsync(ChatMessage, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
      (Run with single chat message)
    
    • RunAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
      (Core method: collection of messages - delegates to RunCoreAsync)
  
  - RunCoreAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
    Primary invocation method that implementations must override
  
  - RunStreamingAsync() - Multiple overloads:
    • RunStreamingAsync(AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>
    • RunStreamingAsync(string, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>
    • RunStreamingAsync(ChatMessage, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>
    • RunStreamingAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>
  
  - RunCoreStreamingAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>
    Primary streaming invocation method that implementations must override

SESSION MANAGEMENT:
  - SerializeSessionAsync(AgentSession, JsonSerializerOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<JsonElement>
    Persist session to JSON
  
  - DeserializeSessionAsync(JsonElement, JsonSerializerOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentSession>
    Restore session from JSON
  
  - GetService(Type, object): object (nullable)
    Retrieve strongly-typed services from agent
  
  - GetService<TService>(object): TService (nullable)
    Generic version of service retrieval

SECURITY NOTES:
  - Messages flow across trust boundaries to external AI services
  - LLM responses treated as untrusted output
  - System messages must be developer-controlled
  - Function tool arguments treated as untrusted input
  - Tool approval requirements recommended for sensitive operations

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 2. AgentResponse (Response Container)
PROPERTIES:
  - Messages (IList<ChatMessage>): Collection of response messages
  - Text (string): Concatenated text from all TextContent items
  - AgentId (string, nullable): ID of agent that generated response
  - ResponseId (string, nullable): Unique response identifier
  - ContinuationToken (ChatClientAgentContinuationToken, nullable): For background responses
  - CreatedAt (DateTimeOffset, nullable): Response creation timestamp
  - FinishReason (ChatFinishReason, nullable): Why response finished (Stop, Length, ToolCalls, etc.)
  - Usage (UsageDetails, nullable): Token counts and resource usage
  - RawRepresentation (object): Underlying implementation object (for debugging)
  - AdditionalProperties (AdditionalPropertiesDictionary, nullable): Custom metadata

CONSTRUCTORS:
  - AgentResponse()
  - AgentResponse(ChatMessage message)
  - AgentResponse(ChatResponse response)
  - AgentResponse(AgentResponse response) - Copy constructor
  - AgentResponse(IList<ChatMessage> messages)

METHODS:
  - ToString(): Returns text representation
  - ToAgentResponseUpdates(): AgentResponseUpdate[] - Convert to streaming format

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 3. AgentResponseUpdate (Streaming Update)
Used for streaming responses - represents individual updates during agent execution

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 4. AgentSession (Conversation State)
PROPERTIES:
  - StateBag (AgentSessionStateBag): Thread-safe key-value store for session state
    ├─ TryGetValue<T>(key, out value, JsonSerializerOptions): bool
    ├─ GetValue<T>(key, JsonSerializerOptions): T
    └─ SetValue<T>(key, value, JsonSerializerOptions): void

CONSTRUCTORS:
  - AgentSession()
  - AgentSession(AgentSessionStateBag stateBag)

METHODS:
  - GetService(Type, object): object (nullable)
  - GetService<TService>(object): TService (nullable)

EXTENSION METHODS (AgentSessionExtensions):
  - TryGetInMemoryChatHistory(session, out messages, stateKey, jsonOptions): bool
    Retrieve in-memory chat history (only for InMemoryChatHistoryProvider)
  
  - SetInMemoryChatHistory(session, messages, stateKey, jsonOptions)
    Set in-memory chat history

SECURITY:

### 6. AIContextProvider (Context Injection)
ABSTRACT BASE CLASS for enhancing AI context during invocation

LIFECYCLE:
  ┌─ InvokingAsync(InvokingContext, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AIContext>
  │  Called at START of agent invocation
  │  Can provide: Context data, tools, instructions, messages
  │
  ├─ [AGENT EXECUTION]
  │
  └─ InvokedAsync(InvokedContext, CancellationToken): ValueTask
     Called at END of agent invocation (even on failure)
     Can: Extract memories, log, audit, perform cleanup

IMPLEMENTATION METHODS TO OVERRIDE:
  - InvokingCoreAsync(): Default filters messages by source, calls ProvideAIContextAsync
  - ProvideAIContextAsync(): Override to provide custom context
  - InvokedCoreAsync(): Default error handling, calls StoreAIContextAsync
  - StoreAIContextAsync(): Override to process results

CONTEXT TYPES:
  - InvokingContext: Request context with messages being sent
  - InvokedContext: Response context with request/response messages and exceptions

PROPERTIES:
  - StateKeys (HashSet<string>): Keys for storing provider state in StateBag
    Default: Single-element set with concrete type name

SECURITY:
  - Providers must be trusted (can inject system messages)
  - External data sources may contain adversarial content
  - Validate and sanitize retrieved data
  - No validation/filtering by framework

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ABSTRACT BASE CLASS for managing conversation history

LIFECYCLE: Same two-phase pattern as AIContextProvider
  - InvokingAsync(): Load chat history at start
  - InvokedAsync(): Persist chat history at end

CONTEXT TYPES:
  - InvokingContext: Request context
  - InvokedContext: Response context

Default implementations available:
  - InMemoryChatHistoryProvider: Stores messages in AgentSession.StateBag
  - (Framework handles persistence for service-managed histories)

## FUNCTION INVOCATION - Microsoft.Extensions.AI v10.5.0

### FunctionInvokingChatClient (Tool Calling Loop)
Location: Microsoft.Extensions.AI namespace
Extends: DelegatingChatClient

Automatically invokes functions in chat responses - CORE OF TOOL CALLING LOOP

CONSTRUCTOR:
  - FunctionInvokingChatClient(
      IChatClient innerClient,
      ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
      IServiceProvider? functionInvocationServices = null
    )

KEY PROPERTIES FOR TOOL CALLING:
  - MaximumIterationsPerRequest (int, default: 40)
    Max roundtrips between agent and service for single request
  
  - MaximumConsecutiveErrorsPerRequest (int, default: 3)
    Max consecutive function invocation failures before terminating
  
  - AllowConcurrentInvocation (bool, default: false)
    Allow parallel function invocation in single response
  
  - AdditionalTools (IList<AITool>): Tools beyond ChatOptions.Tools
  
  - TerminateOnUnknownCalls (bool, default: false)
    Terminate loop if unknown tool called (vs returning error)
  
  - IncludeDetailedErrors (bool, default: false)
    Include full exception details in chat history (security consideration)
  
  - FunctionInvoker (Delegate, nullable):
    Custom function invocation delegate
  
  - CurrentContext (FunctionInvocationContext):
    Current function invocation context (flows across async)

TOOL CALLING LOOP:
  1. Client sends ChatMessage + Tools to inner IChatClient
  2. Response contains FunctionCallContent
  3. FunctionInvokingChatClient invokes matching AIFunction
  4. Wraps result in FunctionResultContent
  5. Sends back to inner client with updated chat history
  6. Repeat 2-5 until no more function calls or max iterations reached
  7. Return final ChatResponse

FUNCTION TYPES:
  - AIFunction: Executable function (FunctionInvokingChatClient invokes automatically)
  - AIFunctionDeclaration: Declaration only (caller handles invocation)
  - ApprovalRequiredAIFunction: Requires approval before invocation
    └─ Replaced with ToolApprovalRequestContent
    └─ Caller responds with ToolApprovalResponseContent
    └─ FunctionInvokingChatClient then invokes

CORE METHODS:
  - GetResponseAsync(messages, options, cancellationToken): ValueTask<ChatResponse>
  - GetStreamingResponseAsync(messages, options, cancellationToken): IAsyncEnumerable<StreamingChatResponseUpdate>

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## MINIMAL AGENT LOOP WITH TOOL CALLING EXAMPLE

```csharp
// 1. Setup
var services = new ServiceCollection()
    .AddLogging()
    .BuildServiceProvider();

var httpClient = new HttpClient();
var chatClient = new OllamaChatClient(httpClient, "llama2");

// 2. Add function invocation loop
chatClient = new FunctionInvokingChatClient(
    chatClient,
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>()
);

// 3. Create agent with chat options containing tools
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Name = "My Assistant",
    Description = "An AI assistant",
    Instructions = "You are a helpful AI assistant.",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new List<AITool> { tool1, tool2 }
    }
};

var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    agentOptions,
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>(),
    services
);

// 4. Create session and run loop
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync();

while (true)
{
    string userInput = Console.ReadLine();
    
    var response = await agent.RunAsync(
        userInput,
        session,
        new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
        CancellationToken.None
    );
    
    Console.WriteLine($"Agent: {response.Text}");
    
    // Session automatically updated with response messages
    // Tool calls handled automatically by FunctionInvokingChatClient
}
```

    └─ Caller responds with ToolApprovalResponseContent
    └─ FunctionInvokingChatClient then invokes

CORE METHODS:
  - GetResponseAsync(messages, options, cancellationToken): ValueTask<ChatResponse>
  - GetStreamingResponseAsync(messages, options, cancellationToken): IAsyncEnumerable<StreamingChatResponseUpdate>

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## MINIMAL AGENT LOOP WITH TOOL CALLING EXAMPLE

```csharp
// 1. Setup
var services = new ServiceCollection()
    .AddLogging()
    .BuildServiceProvider();

var httpClient = new HttpClient();
var chatClient = new OllamaChatClient(httpClient, "llama2");

// 2. Add function invocation loop
chatClient = new FunctionInvokingChatClient(
    chatClient,
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>()
);

// 3. Create agent with chat options containing tools
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Name = "My Assistant",
    Description = "An AI assistant",
    Instructions = "You are a helpful AI assistant.",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new List<AITool> { tool1, tool2 }
    }
};

var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    agentOptions,
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>(),
    services
);

// 4. Create session and run loop
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync();

while (true)
{
    string userInput = Console.ReadLine();
    
    var response = await agent.RunAsync(
        userInput,
        session,
        new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(),
        CancellationToken.None
    );
    
    Console.WriteLine($"Agent: {response.Text}");
    
    // Session automatically updated with response messages
    // Tool calls handled automatically by FunctionInvokingChatClient
}
```

## **Microsoft.Agents.AI Framework - Public API Surface Summary**

### **Core Abstractions** (`Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions` v1.3.0)

#### **1. AIAgent** (Abstract Base Class)
**Key Properties:**
- `Id` (string) - Unique agent identifier
- `Name` (string?) - Human-readable name
- `Description` (string?) - Purpose/capabilities
- `CurrentRunContext` (AgentRunContext) - Current run context (flows across async)

**Core Run Methods:**
```
RunAsync(AgentSession?, AgentRunOptions?, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
RunAsync(string message, ...): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
RunAsync(ChatMessage, ...): ValueTask<AgentResponse>
RunAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, ...): ValueTask<AgentResponse>  ← Main overload
RunCoreAsync(...): ValueTask<AgentResponse>  ← Must override

RunStreamingAsync(...): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>  ← Multiple overloads
RunCoreStreamingAsync(...): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>  ← Must override
```

**Session Management:**
```
CreateSessionAsync(CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentSession>
SerializeSessionAsync(AgentSession, ...): ValueTask<JsonElement>
DeserializeSessionAsync(JsonElement, ...): ValueTask<AgentSession>
GetService<T>(): T?
```

#### **2. AgentResponse** (Response Container)
**Properties:**
- `Messages` (IList<ChatMessage>) - All response messages
- `Text` (string) - Concatenated text content
- `AgentId` (string?) - Generating agent ID
- `FinishReason` (ChatFinishReason?) - Stop, Length, ToolCalls, etc.
- `Usage` (UsageDetails?) - Token counts
- `ContinuationToken` (ChatClientAgentContinuationToken?) - For background responses
- `CreatedAt` (DateTimeOffset?) - Timestamp
- `AdditionalProperties` (AdditionalPropertiesDictionary?) - Custom metadata

#### **3. AgentSession** (Conversation State)
**Key Properties:**
- `StateBag` (AgentSessionStateBag) - Thread-safe session state storage
  - `TryGetValue<T>(key, out value, JsonSerializerOptions): bool`
  - `GetValue<T>(key, options): T`
  - `SetValue<T>(key, value, options): void`
  - `Count` (int) - Number of stored items

**Extension Methods:**
```
TryGetInMemoryChatHistory(session, out messages, stateKey?, options?): bool
SetInMemoryChatHistory(session, messages, stateKey?, options?): void
```

#### **4. AgentRunOptions** (Base Configuration)
**Properties:**
- `AllowBackgroundResponses` (bool)
- `ContinuationToken` (ChatClientAgentContinuationToken?)

#### **5. AIContextProvider** (Context Injection)
**Two-Phase Lifecycle:**
```
┌─ InvokingAsync(InvokingContext, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AIContext>
│  Called at START - provides additional context, tools, instructions
│
├─ [AGENT EXECUTION]
│
└─ InvokedAsync(InvokedContext, CancellationToken): ValueTask
   Called at END (even on failure) - extracts memories, logs, audits
```

**Override Points:**
- `ProvideAIContextAsync()` - Provide context (recommended override)
- `StoreAIContextAsync()` - Store/extract from results (recommended override)
- `InvokingCoreAsync()` - Full control over invoking (advanced)
- `InvokedCoreAsync()` - Full control over invoked (advanced)

**Context Objects:**
- `InvokingContext` - Has: messages, session, options, chat options
- `InvokedContext` - Has: request messages, response messages, exception, session

#### **6. ChatHistoryProvider** (Chat History Management)
Same two-phase lifecycle as AIContextProvider:
- `InvokingAsync()` - Load history at start
- `InvokedAsync()` - Persist history at end

**Default Implementation:** `InMemoryChatHistoryProvider` (stores in session StateBag)

### **Concrete Implementation** (`Microsoft.Agents.AI` v1.3.0)

#### **1. ChatClientAgent** (Main Implementation)
**Extends:** AIAgent
**Implements:** Delegates to IChatClient with automatic tool handling

**Constructors:**

)

- `RunCoreStreamingAsync()` - Implementation of streaming

- `ApplyRunOptionsTransformations()` - Configure client per run

#### **2. ChatClientAgentOptions** (Configuration)

### **Tool Calling Loop** (`Microsoft.Extensions.AI` v10.5.0)

#### **FunctionInvokingChatClient** (Automatic Tool Invocation)
**Extends:** DelegatingChatClient

**Constructor:**
```csharp
FunctionInvokingChatClient(
    IChatClient innerClient,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? functionInvocationServices = null
)
```

**Tool Calling Properties:**
- `MaximumIterationsPerRequest` (int, default: 40) - Max roundtrips
- `MaximumConsecutiveErrorsPerRequest` (int, default: 3) - Consecutive failures allowed
- `AllowConcurrentInvocation` (bool, default: false) - Parallel function calls
- `AdditionalTools` (IList<AITool>) - Beyond ChatOptions.Tools
- `TerminateOnUnknownCalls` (bool, default: false) - Exit on unknown tool
- `IncludeDetailedErrors` (bool, default: false) - Full exception details in history
- `FunctionInvoker` (Delegate?) - Custom invocation handler
- `CurrentContext` (FunctionInvocationContext) - Current invocation state

**Tool Call Loop Process:**
```
1. Client sends ChatMessage + Tools to inner IChatClient
2. Response contains FunctionCallContent
3. FunctionInvokingChatClient finds matching AIFunction
4. Invokes function → FunctionResultContent
5. Sends back to inner client with updated history
6. Repeat steps 2-5 until no more tool calls or max iterations
7. Return final ChatResponse
```

**Function Types:**
- `AIFunction` - Executable (auto-invoked)
- `AIFunctionDeclaration` - Declaration only (manual handling)
- `ApprovalRequiredAIFunction` - Requires approval (→ ToolApprovalRequestContent)

---

### **Minimal Agent Loop Example**

```csharp
// 1. Setup services
var services = new ServiceCollection()
    .AddLogging()
    .BuildServiceProvider();

// 2. Create base chat client
var chatClient = new OllamaChatClient(httpClient, "llama2");

// 3. Wrap with function invocation loop
chatClient = new FunctionInvokingChatClient(
    chatClient,
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>()
);

// 4. Create agent with tools
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Name = "Assistant",
    Instructions = "You are helpful.",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new List<AITool> { myTool1, myTool2 }
    }
};

var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, agentOptions, 
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>(), services);

// 5. Run conversation loop
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync();
while (true)
{
    var userInput = Console.ReadLine();
    var response = await agent.RunAsync(userInput, session, null, CancellationToken.None);
    Console.WriteLine($"Agent: {response.Text}");
    // Session auto-updated, tool calls handled automatically
}
```

---

### **Security Considerations**
- ⚠️ Tool arguments treated as **untrusted input**
- ⚠️ All tools invoked **without approval by default**
- ⚠️ LLM responses are **untrusted output**
- ⚠️ System messages must be **developer-controlled**
- ⚠️ Indirect prompt injection via context providers/tools/history
- ⚠️ Serialized sessions contain **sensitive data** (PII, conversation)

- `CreatedAt` (DateTimeOffset?) - Timestamp
- `AdditionalProperties` (AdditionalPropertiesDictionary?) - Custom metadata

#### **3. AgentSession** (Conversation State)
**Key Properties:**
- `StateBag` (AgentSessionStateBag) - Thread-safe session state storage
  - `TryGetValue<T>(key, out value, JsonSerializerOptions): bool`
  - `GetValue<T>(key, options): T`
  - `SetValue<T>(key, value, options): void`
  - `Count` (int) - Number of stored items

**Extension Methods:**
```
TryGetInMemoryChatHistory(session, out messages, stateKey?, options?): bool
SetInMemoryChatHistory(session, messages, stateKey?, options?): void
```

#### **4. AgentRunOptions** (Base Configuration)
**Properties:**
- `AllowBackgroundResponses` (bool)
- `ContinuationToken` (ChatClientAgentContinuationToken?)

#### **5. AIContextProvider** (Context Injection)
**Two-Phase Lifecycle:**
```
┌─ InvokingAsync(InvokingContext, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AIContext>
│  Called at START - provides additional context, tools, instructions
│
├─ [AGENT EXECUTION]
│
└─ InvokedAsync(InvokedContext, CancellationToken): ValueTask
   Called at END (even on failure) - extracts memories, logs, audits
```

**Override Points:**
- `ProvideAIContextAsync()` - Provide context (recommended override)
- `StoreAIContextAsync()` - Store/extract from results (recommended override)
- `InvokingCoreAsync()` - Full control over invoking (advanced)
- `InvokedCoreAsync()` - Full control over invoked (advanced)

**Context Objects:**
- `InvokingContext` - Has: messages, session, options, chat options
- `InvokedContext` - Has: request messages, response messages, exception, session

#### **6. ChatHistoryProvider** (Chat History Management)
Same two-phase lifecycle as AIContextProvider:
- `InvokingAsync()` - Load history at start
- `InvokedAsync()` - Persist history at end

**Default Implementation:** `InMemoryChatHistoryProvider` (stores in session StateBag)

---

### **Concrete Implementation** (`Microsoft.Agents.AI` v1.3.0)

#### **1. ChatClientAgent** (Main Implementation)
**Extends:** AIAgent
**Implements:** Delegates to IChatClient with automatic tool handling

**Constructors:**
```csharp
ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    string? instructions = null,
    string? name = null,
    string? description = null,
    IList<AITool>? tools = null,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? services = null
)

ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    ChatClientAgentOptions options,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? services = null
)
```

**Key Properties:**
- `ChatClient` (IChatClient) - Underlying decorated chat client
- `ChatHistoryProvider` (ChatHistoryProvider?) - If configured
- `AIContextProviders` (List<AIContextProvider>?) - Additional providers
- `Instructions` (string?)
- `ChatOptions` (ChatOptions) - Default options

**Methods:**
- `RunCoreAsync()` - Implementation of agent execution
- `RunCoreStreamingAsync()` - Implementation of streaming
- `CreateSessionAsync(conversationId)` - Create session for existing conversation
- `ApplyRunOptionsTransformations()` - Configure client per run

#### **2. ChatClientAgentOptions** (Configuration)
**Properties:**
- `Id`, `Name`, `Description` (string?)
- `ChatOptions` (ChatOptions) - Default chat options with tools
- `ChatHistoryProvider` (ChatHistoryProvider?)
- `AIContextProviders` (List<AIContextProvider>?)
- `UseProvidedChatClientAsIs` (bool, default: false) - Skip decorators
- `ClearOnChatHistoryProviderConflict` (bool, default: true)
- `WarnOnChatHistoryProviderConflict` (bool, default: true)
- `ThrowOnChatHistoryProviderConflict` (bool, default: true)
- `RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence` (bool, default: false)

**Methods:**
- `Clone()`: ChatClientAgentOptions

#### **3. ChatClientAgentRunOptions** (Per-Invocation Config)
**Extends:** AgentRunOptions

**Properties:**
- `ChatOptions` (ChatOptions?) - Override for this run
- `ChatClientFactory` (Func<IChatClient, IChatClient>?) - Decorate client per-request

#### **4. ChatClientAgentSession** (Session Implementation)
**Extends:** AgentSession

#### **5. AIAgentBuilder** (Builder Pattern)
**Constructors:**
```csharp
AIAgentBuilder(AIAgent innerAgent)
AIAgentBuilder(Func<IServiceProvider, AIAgent> innerAgentFactory)
```

**Methods:**
```
Build(IServiceProvider? services): AIAgent
Use(Func<AIAgent, AIAgent> factory): AIAgentBuilder
Use(Func<AIAgent, IServiceProvider, AIAgent> factory): AIAgentBuilder
Use(Func<messages, session, options, next, ct, Task> sharedFunc): AIAgentBuilder
Use(Func<...> runFunc, Func<...> runStreamingFunc): AIAgentBuilder
UseAIContextProviders(params MessageAIContextProvider[] providers): AIAgentBuilder
```

---

### **Tool Calling Loop** (`Microsoft.Extensions.AI` v10.5.0)

#### **FunctionInvokingChatClient** (Automatic Tool Invocation)
**Extends:** DelegatingChatClient

**Constructor:**
```csharp
FunctionInvokingChatClient(
    IChatClient innerClient,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? functionInvocationServices = null
)
```

**Tool Calling Properties:**
- `MaximumIterationsPerRequest` (int, default: 40) - Max roundtrips
- `MaximumConsecutiveErrorsPerRequest` (int, default: 3) - Consecutive failures allowed
- `AllowConcurrentInvocation` (bool, default: false) - Parallel function calls
- `AdditionalTools` (IList<AITool>) - Beyond ChatOptions.Tools
- `TerminateOnUnknownCalls` (bool, default: false) - Exit on unknown tool
- `IncludeDetailedErrors` (bool, default: false) - Full exception details in history
- `FunctionInvoker` (Delegate?) - Custom invocation handler
- `CurrentContext` (FunctionInvocationContext) - Current invocation state

**Tool Call Loop Process:**
```
1. Client sends ChatMessage + Tools to inner IChatClient
2. Response contains FunctionCallContent
3. FunctionInvokingChatClient finds matching AIFunction
4. Invokes function → FunctionResultContent
5. Sends back to inner client with updated history
6. Repeat steps 2-5 until no more tool calls or max iterations
7. Return final ChatResponse
```

**Function Types:**
- `AIFunction` - Executable (auto-invoked)
- `AIFunctionDeclaration` - Declaration only (manual handling)
- `ApprovalRequiredAIFunction` - Requires approval (→ ToolApprovalRequestContent)

---

### **Minimal Agent Loop Example**

```csharp
// 1. Setup services
var services = new ServiceCollection()
    .AddLogging()
    .BuildServiceProvider();

// 2. Create base chat client
var chatClient = new OllamaChatClient(httpClient, "llama2");

// 3. Wrap with function invocation loop
chatClient = new FunctionInvokingChatClient(
    chatClient,
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>()
);

// 4. Create agent with tools
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Name = "Assistant",
    Instructions = "You are helpful.",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new List<AITool> { myTool1, myTool2 }
    }
};

var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, agentOptions, 
    services.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>(), services);

// 5. Run conversation loop
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync();
while (true)
{
    var userInput = Console.ReadLine();
    var response = await agent.RunAsync(userInput, session, null, CancellationToken.None);
    Console.WriteLine($"Agent: {response.Text}");
    // Session auto-updated, tool calls handled automatically
}
```

---

### **Security Considerations**
- ⚠️ Tool arguments treated as **untrusted input**
- ⚠️ All tools invoked **without approval by default**
- ⚠️ LLM responses are **untrusted output**
- ⚠️ System messages must be **developer-controlled**
- ⚠️ Indirect prompt injection via context providers/tools/history
- ⚠️ Serialized sessions contain **sensitive data** (PII, conversation)

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the API surface. Let me build the research agent app. Here's my plan:

1. Create tools (web search + note-taking) as `AIFunction`s
2. Set up an OpenAI `IChatClient` via `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI`
3. Wrap it with `FunctionInvokingChatClient` for automatic tool invocation
4. Create a `ChatClientAgent` with instructions, tools, and the client
5. Run the agent loop

For the web search tool, I'll simulate web search results since we don't have a real search API key. Actually, let me use `web_fetch` or a real approach. 

I'm thinking about this more practically—since this is a console demo app, I'll create a web search tool using HttpClient to call an actual search API, or simulate realistic search results. I'll also build a note-taking tool that keeps notes in memory, then wire both up with the ChatClientAgent.

For the implementation, I'll use environment variables to configure the OpenAI API key and endpoint, then write out the complete Program.cs file...

Writing the console app code... Implementing the web search function... Continuing with the note retrieval method...

Now I'm implementing a parser for the search results that extracts links and snippets from the HTML using regex patterns to match the DuckDuckGo lite format. Finishing the snippet trimming, then adding validation to ensure the title isn't empty before storing results. Now I'm wrapping up the scraper class and moving into the main program setup, checking for the OpenAI API key environment variable and defaulting to a model if one isn't specified. Setting up the agent instructions with a research workflow that includes web searching, note-taking, and summary generation, then displaying a formatted console header for the research agent interface.

Building the main interaction loop... Checking the ChatClientAgent constructor signatures to understand which overload to use and whether Instructions is settable through the options. I'm seeing that `Instructions` is actually a property on the `ChatClientAgent` itself, not on the options. So I can use the simpler constructor that takes the chat client, instructions, name, description, and tools as parameters directly.

The key insight is that by default `UseProvidedChatClientAsIs` is false, which means the agent automatically wraps the client with decorators including `FunctionInvokingChatClient` to handle the tool calling loop—so I don't need to manually wrap it myself. I need to figure out whether passing tools to the constructor automatically puts them in `ChatOptions.Tools`, and then I'm checking how to properly instantiate the OpenAI client using the Microsoft.Extensions.AI bridge - specifically whether there's a simpler constructor or if I need to use `AsIChatClient()` on the standard pattern.

For the implementation, I'm keeping it straightforward with just OpenAI support for now, though I could add Azure OpenAI support later via environment variables. I'm structuring this with static tool methods that work with `AIFunction`, then building out the main program with `ChatClientAgent`.

One thing I need to verify: when I pass tools to `ChatClientAgent`, does it automatically handle the function invocation loop? Based on the documentation, the agent decorates the provided `IChatClient` by default with additional behavior like `FunctionInvokingChatClient`, so I should just pass the raw client and let it manage everything. I should also be explicit about using `InMemoryChatHistoryProvider` for the chat history, and configure the agent options with the name, description, and tools.

Actually, I'm reconsidering the approach. The simpler constructor that takes `instructions`, `name`, `description`, and `tools` directly might be cleaner since each research run is essentially a single request. The tool calling loop happens automatically within a single `RunAsync` call—when the model responds with tool calls, the `FunctionInvokingChatClient` invokes them and sends results back, looping until the model gives a final response, all within one `CompleteAsync` call.

So the flow is: user sends a research request, `RunAsync` gets called, the model decides what tools to use, the client invokes them and feeds results back, the model chains multiple tool calls together, and finally returns a summary—all in one go. This is exactly what we need for autonomous research. Let me write the implementation now.

For the web search, I'll use DuckDuckGo's HTML lite endpoint since it's straightforward. I need to verify the `AsIChatClient()` extension method from the OpenAI package—it should be available on the ChatClient type in the v2.x release.

Setting up the client... For the tools, I need to wrap them as `AIFunction`s using `AIFunctionFactory.Create()`, and since the notes tool needs to maintain state, I'll create a `ResearchTools` class with instance methods that have `[Description]` attributes on their parameters. Then I'll instantiate it and pass each method to the factory to build the tools array.

Setting up the final implementation... Setting up the agent with web search, note-taking, and note retrieval tools, then configuring it with instructions to conduct thorough research by gathering information from multiple sources and organizing findings into a structured summary.

Building the console UI... Implementing the research tools class... Finishing the error handling and then defining methods to save and retrieve research notes, building a note management system that stores findings and displays them in a formatted list.

Now I'm writing code to parse HTML search results using regex patterns to extract links and snippets... Finishing up the snippet extraction and adding the results to the collection, then I'm defining a helper method to strip HTML tags and decode entities. I'm considering whether to use `GeneratedRegex` for .NET 10, but keeping it simple with regular `Regex` to avoid the complexity of partial classes and source generators. Now I'm looking at the `RunAsync` overload signature to understand the API better. I'm checking the method signature for `RunAsync` with a string parameter to see what other arguments it accepts. Looking at the exploration data, it appears the overload takes the message, an optional session, optional run options, and a cancellation token. I need to verify whether those parameters have defaults or if I need to explicitly pass null values to make the call work cleanly. Writing the final code...

data.content: Build succeeded with zero warnings/errors. Let me verify the final file looks clean:

The app builds cleanly. Here's what it does:

**Architecture** — uses `ChatClientAgent` from `Microsoft.Agents.AI` with three tools registered via `AIFunctionFactory`:

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `SearchWeb` | Queries DuckDuckGo HTML lite, parses results into titles/URLs/snippets |
| `SaveNote` | Stores findings in an in-memory list |
| `GetNotes` | Retrieves all saved notes for summarization |

**Agent loop** — the `ChatClientAgent` automatically wraps the `IChatClient` with `FunctionInvokingChatClient`, so when the model emits tool calls, they're executed and results fed back until the model produces a final text summary. One `RunAsync` call handles the full multi-step research cycle.

**To run:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
export OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini   # optional, defaults to gpt-4o-mini
dotnet run --project ResearchAgent
```
